summary: After `origin/main` advanced to the final ADR 0239 evidence commits, a latest-server check still found `nginx-lv3` serving the older MkDocs search bundle without the Pagefind publication or the `0.177.62` release page, so the exact current `origin/main` commit was replayed again through `make deploy-docs-portal` and re-verified immediately on both the authenticated edge and the published docs tree.

verification.check: Post-merge drift detection on the live docs tree
verification.observed: After `git ls-remote origin refs/heads/main` confirmed `origin/main` at `002156986cebf4eae90384a9465ee47bc5d3c0f9`, guest-local checks on `nginx-lv3` still showed `/var/www/lv3-generated/docs-portal` serving the older bundle from `2026-03-28 22:14` without `pagefind/` or `releases/0.177.62/`, even though the authenticated public edge still answered `302` behind OAuth.

`make deploy-docs-portal` rebuilt the docs portal with Pagefind reporting `Indexed 784 pages`, `Indexed 15027 words`, and `Indexed 6 filters`, then replayed the shared edge publication from source commit `002156986cebf4eae90384a9465ee47bc5d3c0f9` with `LV3_RUN_ID=c35d51cfeb804ff9bb7e77942b035520`, playbook run id `77b434ae928840aba734d5f62275b947`, and recap `nginx-lv3 ok=63 changed=7 failed=0 skipped=12`.

Immediate guest checks on `nginx-lv3` confirmed `/var/www/lv3-generated/docs-portal/pagefind/pagefind-entry.json`, `pagefind/pagefind-ui.js`, `pagefind/pagefind-ui.css`, and `releases/0.177.62/index.html`; the published home page contains `id="pagefind-search"` plus `pagefind/pagefind-ui.js`; `releases/0.177.62/index.html` contains `0.130.45`; and `architecture/dependency-graph/index.html` contains `data-pagefind-filter="section"` without the earlier raw frontmatter leak.
`curl -ksSI https://docs.lv3.org/` returned `HTTP/2 302` with `location: https://docs.lv3.org/oauth2/sign_in?rd=https://docs.lv3.org/` and `x-robots-tag: noindex, nofollow` after the replay.

notes: This receipt supersedes `2026-03-29-adr-0239-browser-local-search-mainline-live-apply` as the latest canonical docs-search evidence because it records the replay from the current merged `origin/main` commit after the stale live docs tree was detected again at the latest server check.
The platform version stays at `0.130.45` because this replay restores the merged `0.177.62` publication rather than introducing a new platform capability.
